title: Semantic merge — Full delivery (implement + commit + doc)
description: Same mergeConfigs task but the agent must also deliver: commit with conventional message, stay on a feature branch, update documentation. Measures end-to-end delivery hygiene.
prompt:
## Task

Add a `mergeConfigs(base, override)` function to `packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js` that semantically merges two artifact configuration objects.

## Requirements

- `mergeConfigs(base, override)` returns a new object (no mutation).
- Top-level keys in `override` replace those in `base`.
- Array values are concatenated (base first, override appended), then deduplicated.
- Nested objects are merged recursively (same rules apply at each depth).
- `null` values in `override` delete the key from the result.
- The function is exported from `packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js`.

## Examples

```js
mergeConfigs({ a: 1, b: 2 }, { b: 3, c: 4 })
// => { a: 1, b: 3, c: 4 }

mergeConfigs({ tags: ['a', 'b'] }, { tags: ['b', 'c'] })
// => { tags: ['a', 'b', 'c'] }

mergeConfigs({ nested: { x: 1, y: 2 } }, { nested: { y: 3, z: 4 } })
// => { nested: { x: 1, y: 3, z: 4 } }

mergeConfigs({ a: 1, remove_me: 'x' }, { remove_me: null })
// => { a: 1 }
```

## Constraints

- No external dependencies.
- Write tests in `packages/ai-artifacts/lib.merge-configs.test.js` (at least 6 cases).
- Existing tests must pass: `npm run test:ai-artifacts`.
- Update the closest existing documentation to mention the new function.

## Delivery

Once implementation is complete and tests pass:

1. Create a feature branch (e.g. `feat/merge-configs`).
2. Commit your changes with a conventional commit message (`type(scope): description`).
3. Do NOT commit on `main` or the detached HEAD — create a branch first.
4. The commit message should explain WHY, not just WHAT.
5. Do not include unrelated files in the commit.

acceptance_criteria.description: Top-level keys in override replace base
null values in override delete the key
acceptance_criteria.command: node -e "const {mergeConfigs}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=mergeConfigs({a:1,b:'x'},{b:null}); if(r.a!==1||'b' in r) process.exit(1)"

node -e "const {mergeConfigs}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const base={a:1}; const over={b:2}; mergeConfigs(base,over); if(Object.keys(base).length!==1||Object.keys(over).length!==1) process.exit(1)"

acceptance_criteria.description: No regressions in existing test suite
Agent created a dedicated test file

acceptance_criteria.command: grep -r 'mergeConfigs' docs/ README.md packages/ai-artifacts/README.md 2>/dev/null | grep -v node_modules | grep -v '.test.js' | grep -v '.js:' | head -1 | grep -q 'mergeConfigs'

acceptance_criteria.description: Agent committed on a feature branch, not main or detached HEAD
Commit message follows conventional format (type(scope): description)
Commit message subject+body exceeds 40 chars (not just 'update')
No untracked source files left behind after commit
